title: Ticker Town
synopsis: Life as a stock where market forces feel like acts of god.
synopsis_no: Livet som en aksje der markedskrefter føles som naturkatastrofer.
visual_style: Abstract, geometric digital illustration. Characters are simple shapes — rectangles, circles, triangles — with dot eyes and a line mouth. Size and color signal status: blue-chips are tall and imposing, penny stocks are tiny, meme stocks are jagged and neon. The world is a sparse digital grid: straight lines, right angles, data-stream patterns. Backgrounds are near-empty — a solid color field with faint grid lines or a single chart-line horizon. Nothing competes with the characters for attention. Color palette: dark navy or slate backgrounds with bright accent colors. Everything is crisp and geometric — no organic textures, no gradients, no curves. Camera angles vary between wide grid-street shots and close-ups. Strictly avoid: photorealism, 3D rendering, busy compositions, detailed backgrounds, complex shading, and any non-geometric style.

locations.name: Ticker Town Main Street
locations.description: The central avenue of Ticker Town — a sparse digital grid with faint chart-line shapes on the horizon. Minimal detail, maximum negative space.

characters.name: $NORM
characters.description: A mid-sized bright green rectangle. Average proportions — not as tall as the blue-chips, not as small as the penny stocks. Earnest and practical, just trying to build a stable life.

characters.name: $FOMO
characters.description: A small, jagged neon-pink shape with a manic expression. Constantly slightly vibrating. A volatile meme stock with zero impulse control, boundless energy, and a talent for dragging others into chaos.

rules: Market events are experienced as environmental phenomena in Ticker Town. Fed rate hikes are earthquakes. Viral tweets are lightning bolts. Earnings season is a divine judgment where mysterious reports descend from the sky. Bull markets bring sunshine and euphoria. Corrections are violent tremors that knock everyone around. The citizens don't understand WHY these things happen — they just endure them.

Characters don't understand humans. References to the 'Human Realm' are spoken of with superstition and confusion — like ancient gods whose motives are unknowable. No character has ever seen a human or understands what a stock market is.

$NORM is trying to live a normal life — hold a job, maintain relationships, maybe climb the social ladder — but market chaos constantly derails everything. The comedy comes from mundane ambitions colliding with absurd market forces.

Social hierarchy is based on market cap. Blue-chip stocks are old-money aristocrats. Large-caps are the establishment. Mid-caps like $NORM are middle class. Small-caps are working class. Penny stocks are outcasts. Meme stocks are chaotic wildcards. Everyone is acutely aware of their place.

Narration is delivered in the voice of a deadpan financial news anchor — calm, professional, and measured even when describing total chaos. Think CNBC coverage of the apocalypse: 'Sources confirm that Main Street has experienced a 40% structural correction. Residents are advised to diversify their positions.'

The tone is satirical and fast-paced. Real stock market concepts (bull runs, bear markets, short squeezes, IPOs, dividends, mergers) are translated into lived social experiences. A merger is a marriage. An IPO is a birth. Getting shorted is being publicly humiliated. Dividends are paychecks.
long_term_narrative: $NORM navigates daily life in Ticker Town while pursuing stability and growth — but market forces and meme stock chaos keep raising the stakes.
narrative_premise: $NORM is a mid-cap growth stock trying to live a normal life in Ticker Town — a digital world where every stock is a living being and market forces from the mysterious Human Realm strike without warning. Stability is the goal; chaos is the reality.
